static constexpr int max_buckets = 10000
 

Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets()

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

◆ augment_ng_memories()

void BucketGraph::augment_ng_memories ( std::vector< double > & solution,
std::vector< Path > & paths,
bool aggressive,
int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 274 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

275 {
276 std::set<std::pair<int, int>> forbidden_augmentations;
277 std::vector<std::vector<int>> cycles;
278
279 for (int col = 0; col < paths.size(); ++col) {
280
281 if (solution[col] > 1e-2 && solution[col] < 1 - 1e-2) {
282 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, int> visited_clients;
283 std::vector<int> cycle;
284 bool has_cycle = false;
285
286 for (int i = 0; i < paths[col].size(); ++i) {
287 int client = paths[col][i];
288 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) {
289 continue; // Ignore 0 in cycle detection
290 }
291 if (visited_clients.find(client) != visited_clients.end()) {
292 has_cycle = true;
293 // Start from the first occurrence of the repeated client to form the cycle
294 for (int j = visited_clients[client]; j <= i; ++j) { cycle.push_back(paths[col][j]); }
295 break; // Stop once the cycle is stored
296 }
297 visited_clients[client] = i;
298 }
299
300 if (has_cycle) { cycles.push_back(cycle); }
301 }
302 }
303
304 // Sort cycles by size to prioritize smaller cycles
305 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(),
306 [](const std::vector<int> &a, const std::vector<int> &b) { return a.size() < b.size(); });
307 int forbidden_count = 0;
308
309 for (const auto &cycle : cycles) {
310 // Check the current sizes of neighborhoods involved in the cycle
311 bool can_forbid = true;
312 for (const auto &node : cycle) {
313 // Count the number of 1s in neighborhoods_bitmap[node]
314 int count = 0;
315 // print size of neighborhoods_bitmap
316 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
317 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment); // Counts the number of set bits (1s)
318 if (count >= eta_max) {
319 can_forbid = false;
320 break;
321 }
322 }
323 if (!can_forbid) { break; }
324 }
325
326 if (can_forbid && (cycle.size() <= eta1 || (forbidden_count < eta2 && !cycle.empty()))) {
327 // Forbid the cycle
328 forbidCycle(cycle, aggressive);
329 forbidden_count++;
330 }
331
332 if (forbidden_count >= eta2) { break; }
333 }
334}

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::bi_labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the bi-labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

440 {
441 // Stage-specific initializations
442 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three) {
443 heuristic_fixing<S>();
444 } else if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
445 if (first_reset) {
446 reset_fixed();
447 first_reset = false;
448 }
449#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
450 if (options.bucket_fixing) {
451 bucket_fixing<S>();
452 }
453#endif
454 }
455
456 reset_pool();
457 common_initialization();
458
459 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size());
460 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size());
461
462 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
463 run_labeling_algorithms<S, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
464 } else {
465 forward_cbar =
466 labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, S, Full::Partial>();
467 }
468
469 auto best_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
470 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
471 if (check_feasibility(fw_best_label, bw_best_label)) {
472 best_label = compute_label<S>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
473 } else {
474 best_label->cost = 0.0;
475 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
476 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
477 }
478 } else {
479 best_label->cost = best_label->real_cost;
480 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
481 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
482 }
483 merged_labels.push_back(best_label);
484
485 if constexpr (S == Stage::Enumerate) {
486 fmt::print("Labels generated, concatenating...\n");
487 }
488
489 const size_t n_segments = (fw_buckets_size + 63) / 64;
490 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments);
491
492 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
493 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < fw_buckets_size; ++bucket) {
494 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
495
496 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
497 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket += bucket_labels.size();
498 }
499
500 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
501 const auto &to_arcs =
502 nodes[L->node_id].get_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
503
504 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
505 const int to_node = arc.to;
506
507 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
508 if (fixed_arcs[L->node_id][to_node] == 1) {
509 continue;
510 }
511 }
512
513 auto extended_labels =
514 Extend<Direction::Forward, S, ArcType::Node,
515 Mutability::Const, Full::Reverse>(L, arc);
516 for (Label *L_prime : extended_labels) {
517 int bucket_to_process =
518 L_prime->vertex; // Create mutable copy
519 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
520 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
521 ConcatenateLabel<S, SYM>(L, bucket_to_process, best_cost,
522 Bvisited);
523 }
524 }
525 }
526 }
527
528 pdqsort(merged_labels.begin(), merged_labels.end(),
529 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
530
531#ifdef SCHRODINGER
532 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
533 std::vector<Path> paths;
534 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();
535 const int end_idx = std::min(N_ADD + N_ADD, labels_size);
536
537 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);
538 for (int i = N_ADD; i < end_idx; ++i) {
539 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
540 continue;
541 }
542 paths.emplace_back(merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered,
543 merged_labels[i]->real_cost);
544 }
545
546 sPool.add_paths(paths);
547 sPool.iterate();
548 }
549#endif
550
551#ifdef RIH
552 std::vector<Label *> top_labels;
553 top_labels.reserve(N_ADD);
554
555 const int n_candidates =
556 std::min(N_ADD, static_cast<int>(merged_labels.size()));
557 for (int i = 0; i < n_candidates; ++i) {
558 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) {
559 continue;
560 }
561 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
562 }
563 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
564#endif
565
566 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
567 return merged_labels;
568}

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 775 of file BucketUtils.h.

775 {
776 // Stage 4 bucket arc fixing
777 if (!fixed) {
778 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE \033[0m");
779 fmt::print("\n");
780 fixed = true;
781 common_initialization();
782
783 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
784 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
785
786 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
787
788 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
789
790 // check if gap is -inf and early exit, due to IPM
791 if (gap < 0) {
792 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO GAP\033[0m");
793 fmt::print("\n");
794 // print the gap
795 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
796 return;
797 }
798 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
799 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
800
801 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
802
803 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
804 work, bi_sched,
805 SECTION {
806 // Section 1: Forward direction
807 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
808 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
809 },
810 SECTION {
811 // Section 2: Backward direction
812 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
813 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
814 });
815
816 generate_arcs();
817 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m");
818 fmt::print("\n");
819 just_fixed = true;
820 return;
821 }
822 just_fixed = false;
823}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

Definition at line 133 of file BucketJump.h.

133 {
134 // Assign forward or backward buckets, adjacency lists, and fixed buckets based on direction
135 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
136 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
137 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
138 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
139 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
140
141 // Reset fixed_buckets
142 for (auto &fb : fixed_buckets) { std::fill(fb.begin(), fb.end(), 0); }
143
144 using ArcMap = ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>, ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
145 arc_map_hash>;
146
147 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
148 int removed_arcs = 0;
149
150 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) { return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b); };
151
152 // Function to process jump arcs
153 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
154 const auto &jump_arcs = buckets[b].template get_jump_arcs<D>();
155 if (!jump_arcs.empty()) {
156 const size_t n_segments = buckets_size / 64 + 1;
157 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
158 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
159 for (const auto &a : jump_arcs) {
160 auto increment = a.resource_increment;
161
162 // auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket), b);
163 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket, b);
164 int b_opposite = get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.jump_bucket, increment);
165
166 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
167
168 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
169 Bidi_map.insert(b);
170 if (!Bvisited.empty()) { std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0, Bvisited.size() * sizeof(uint64_t)); }
171 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite, Bvisited);
172 }
173 }
174 }
175 };
176
177 // Function to process bucket arcs
178 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
179 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
180 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
181
182 const size_t n_segments = buckets_size / 64 + 1;
183 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
184
185 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
186 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0);
187 // print increment.size
188 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
189 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
190 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
191 } else {
192 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
193 }
194 }
195 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id, buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
196 int b_opposite = get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
197 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
198
199 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
200 Bidi_map.insert(b);
201 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0, Bvisited.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
202 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite, Bvisited);
203 }
204
205 // Check for opposite direction arcs
206 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(arc_key); it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
207 auto &Bidi_map_opposite = it->second;
208 bool contains =
209 std::any_of(Phi_opposite[b_opposite].begin(), Phi_opposite[b_opposite].end(), [&](int b_prime) {
210 return Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_prime) != Bidi_map_opposite.end();
211 });
212
213 if (!contains && Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_opposite) == Bidi_map_opposite.end()) {
214 fixed_buckets[a.from_bucket][a.to_bucket] = 1;
215 ++removed_arcs;
216 }
217 }
218 }
219 };
220
221 // Process each bucket
222 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
223 const auto &node_arcs = nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
224
225 // Process node arcs
226 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
227 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
228 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
229
230 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
231 Bidi_map.insert(b);
232 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
233 auto neighbor_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
234 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key); it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
235 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
236 }
237 }
238 }
239 }
240
241 // Process jump and bucket arcs using separate functions
242 process_jump_arcs(b);
243 process_bucket_arcs(b);
244 }
245
246 // Print the number of arcs removed
247 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
248 print_info("[Fw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
249 } else {
250 print_info("[Bw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
251 }
252}

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 224 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

224 {
225 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
226
227 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmaps as vectors of uint64_t for forward and backward neighborhoods
228 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Forward neighborhood
229
230 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
231 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>> forward_distances; // Distances for forward neighbors
232
233 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
234 if (i != j) {
235 // Forward distance (i -> j)
236 double forward_distance = getcij(i, j);
237 forward_distances.push_back({forward_distance, j});
238 }
239 }
240
241 // Sort distances to find the closest nodes
242 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end());
243
244 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap vector for node i (forward and backward)
245 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
246 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0); // Resizing for forward bitmap
247
248 // Include the node itself in both forward and backward neighborhoods
249 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
250 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
251 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self); // Forward
252
253 // Map the top 'num_closest' closest nodes for forward and set them in the backward neighborhoods
254 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size(); ++k) {
255 size_t node_index = forward_distances[k].second;
256
257 // Determine the segment and the bit within the segment for the node_index (forward)
258 size_t segment = node_index >> 6;
259 size_t bit_position = node_index & 63;
260 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position); // Forward neighbor
261 }
262 }
263}

double getcij(int i, int j) const

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 759 of file BucketGraph.h.

759 {
760 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
761
762 // Cache resources and node data
763 const auto &fw_resources = fw_label->resources;
764 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
765 const struct VRPNode &VRPNode = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
766
767 // Time feasibility check
768 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
769 if (options.resources[r] != "time") { continue; }
770 const auto time_fw = fw_resources[r];
771 const auto time_bw = bw_resources[r];
772 const auto travel_time = getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
773 if (time_fw + travel_time + VRPNode.duration > time_bw) { return false; }
774 }
775
776 // Resource feasibility check (if applicable)
777 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
778 for (size_t i = 1; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
779 const auto resource_fw = fw_resources[i];
780 const auto resource_bw = bw_resources[i];
781 const auto demand = VRPNode.demand;
782
783 if (resource_fw + demand > resource_bw) { return false; }
784 }
785 }
786
787 return true;
788 }

◆ common_initialization()

void BucketGraph::common_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph by clearing previous data and setting up forward and backward buckets.

Definition at line 465 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

465 {
466 // Clear previous data
467
468 std::vector<Label *> fw_warm_labels;
469 std::vector<Label *> bw_warm_labels;
470
471 if (merged_labels.size() > 0 && options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
472 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
473 work, bi_sched,
474 SECTION_CUSTOM(this, &fw_warm_labels) {
475 fw_warm_labels.clear();
476 fw_warm_labels.reserve(fw_buckets_size);
477 for (auto bucket = 0; bucket < fw_buckets_size; ++bucket) {
478 auto &current_bucket = fw_buckets[bucket];
479 if (auto *label = current_bucket.get_best_label()) { fw_warm_labels.push_back(std::move(label)); }
480 }
481 },
482 SECTION_CUSTOM(this, &bw_warm_labels) {
483 bw_warm_labels.clear();
484 bw_warm_labels.reserve(bw_buckets_size);
485 for (auto bucket = 0; bucket < bw_buckets_size; ++bucket) {
486 auto &current_bucket = bw_buckets[bucket];
487 if (auto *label = current_bucket.get_best_label()) { bw_warm_labels.push_back(std::move(label)); }
488 }
489 });
490 }
491
492 merged_labels.clear();
493 merged_labels.reserve(50);
494 fw_c_bar.clear();
495 bw_c_bar.clear();
496
497 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
498 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
499
500 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
501 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
502 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
503
504 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
505 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
506
507 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
508 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
509 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
510
511 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
512
513 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
514 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
515 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
516 }
517
518 // Clear forward and backward buckets
519 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
520 fw_buckets[b].clear();
521 bw_buckets[b].clear();
522 }
523
524 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
525 // sort the warm labels for the ones with the smallest cost
526 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
527 work, bi_sched,
528 SECTION_CUSTOM(this, &fw_warm_labels) {
529 // Sort forward labels
530 pdqsort(fw_warm_labels.begin(), fw_warm_labels.end(),
531 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
532
533 // Create a new vector for processed labels
534 std::vector<Label *> processed_labels;
535 const size_t process_size =
536 fw_warm_labels.size() > options.n_warm_start ? options.n_warm_start : fw_warm_labels.size();
537 processed_labels.reserve(process_size);
538
539 // Process labels
540 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
541 auto label = fw_warm_labels[i];
542 if (!label->fresh) { continue; }
543
544 // compute_red_cost returns a new label from the pool
545 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(label, true);
546 if (new_label != nullptr) {
547 // Add to bucket first since we have a valid label
548 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_label(new_label);
549 processed_labels.push_back(new_label);
550 }
551 }
552
553 // Replace original vector contents
554 fw_warm_labels.clear(); // Clear without deleting original labels
555 fw_warm_labels = std::move(processed_labels);
556 },
557 SECTION_CUSTOM(this, &bw_warm_labels) {
558 // Sort backward labels
559 pdqsort(bw_warm_labels.begin(), bw_warm_labels.end(),
560 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
561
562 // Create a new vector for processed labels
563 std::vector<Label *> processed_labels;
564 const size_t process_size =
565 bw_warm_labels.size() > options.n_warm_start ? options.n_warm_start : bw_warm_labels.size();
566 processed_labels.reserve(process_size);
567
568 // Process labels
569 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
570 auto label = bw_warm_labels[i];
571 if (!label->fresh) { continue; }
572
573 // compute_red_cost returns a new label from the pool
574 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(label, false);
575 if (new_label != nullptr) {
576 // Add to bucket first since we have a valid label
577 bw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_label(new_label);
578 processed_labels.push_back(new_label);
579 }
580 }
581
582 // Replace original vector contents
583 bw_warm_labels.clear(); // Clear without deleting original labels
584 bw_warm_labels = std::move(processed_labels);
585 });
586 }
587 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
588 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
589
590 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
591 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
592
593 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper bounds
594 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
595 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
596 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
597 }
598 int offset_fw = 0;
599 int offset_bw = 0;
600
601 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across combinations
602 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
603 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
604 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
605 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
606 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
607 int calculated_index_base = is_forward ? options.depot : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
608
609 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
610 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
611
612 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
613 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
614 if (depth == num_intervals) {
615 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current combination
616 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
617 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
618
619 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval starts or ends
620 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
621 interval_bounds[r] =
622 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] + current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
623 : interval_ends[r] - current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r]);
624 }
625
626 // print interval
627 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
628 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds, depot_id);
629 depot->is_extended = false;
630 //depot->nodes_covered.push_back(depot_id);
631 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
632 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
633 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
634 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
635
636 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
637 return;
638 }
639
640 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
641 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
642 current_pos[depth] = k;
643 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
644 }
645 };
646
647 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
648 generate_combinations(0);
649 };
650
651 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
652 initialize_intervals_combinations(false); // Backward direction
653}

#define SECTION_CUSTOM(capture_list...)

◆ compute_mono_label()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

Computes the mono label for the BucketGraph.

This function computes the mono label for the BucketGraph by acquiring a new label from the label pool and setting the cost and real cost values from the given label L. It then calculates the number of nodes covered by the label and its ancestors, reserves space for the nodes covered, and inserts the nodes from the

Label * BucketGraph::compute_red_cost ( Label * L,

957 {
958 double real_cost = 0.0;
959 double red_cost = 0.0;
960
961 // Initialize an empty SRCmap for the current label
962 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->size(), 0.0);
963
964 int last_node = -1;
965 if (L->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) { return nullptr; }
966 auto new_label = new Label();
967
968 // Traverse through the label nodes from current to root
969 for (auto node_id : L->nodes_covered) {
970
972 // NG-route feasibility check
975 if (is_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id)) { return nullptr; }
976
977 size_t limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size();
978 for (size_t i = 0; i < limit; ++i) {
979 uint64_t current_visited = new_label->visited_bitmap[i]; // Get visited nodes in the current segment
980
981 if (!current_visited) continue; // Skip if no nodes were visited in this segment
982
983 uint64_t neighborhood_mask =
984 neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i]; // Get neighborhood mask for the current node
985 uint64_t bits_to_clear = current_visited & neighborhood_mask; // Determine which bits to clear
986
987 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] = bits_to_clear; // Clear irrelevant visited nodes
988 }
989 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id); // Mark the new node as visited
990
991 if (last_node != -1) {
992 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
993 real_cost += cij_cost;
994 red_cost += cij_cost;
995 }
996
997 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
998
1000 // SRC logic
1003 size_t segment = node_id >> 6; // Determine the segment in the bitmap
1004 size_t bit_position = node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position in the segment
1005
1006 // Update the SRCmap for each cut
1007 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1008 auto &SRCDuals = cutter->SRCDuals;
1009 const uint64_t bit_mask = 1ULL << bit_position;
1010
1011 for (std::size_t idx = 0; idx < cutter->size(); ++idx) {
1012 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
1013 const auto &baseSet = cut.baseSet;
1014 const auto &baseSetorder = cut.baseSetOrder;
1015 const auto &neighbors = cut.neighbors;
1016 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
1017
1018#if defined(SRC)
1019 // Apply SRC logic to update the SRCmap
1020 const bool bitIsSet = neighbors[segment] & bit_mask;
1021
1022 auto &src_map_value = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1023 if (!bitIsSet) {
1024 src_map_value = 0.0; // Reset if bit is not set in neighbors
1025 continue;
1026 }
1027 const bool bitIsSet2 = baseSet[segment] & bit_mask;
1028
1029 if (bitIsSet2) {
1030 auto &den = multipliers.den;
1031 src_map_value += multipliers.num[baseSetorder[node_id]];
1032 if (src_map_value >= den) {
1033 red_cost -= SRCDuals[idx]; // Apply the SRC dual value if threshold is exceeded
1034 src_map_value -= den; // Reset the SRC map value
1035 }
1036 }
1037#endif
1038 }
1039
1040 // Adjust for arc duals if in Stage::Four
1041#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1042 if (last_node) {
1043 auto arc_dual = arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id);
1044 red_cost -= arc_dual;
1045 }
1046#endif
1047
1048 // Adjust for branching duals if they exist
1049 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node) { red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id); }
1050
1051 // Move to the parent node and update last_node
1052 last_node = node_id;
1053 }
1054
1055 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1056 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1057 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1058 new_label->node_id = L->node_id;
1059 new_label->nodes_covered = L->nodes_covered;
1060 new_label->is_extended = false;
1061 new_label->resources = L->resources;
1062 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1063
1064 // Bucket number for the new label
1065 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1066 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) { new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i]; }
1067 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1068 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1069 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1070
1071 new_label->fresh = false;
1072
1073 return new_label;
1074 };

◆ computePhi()

std::vector< int > BucketGraph::computePhi ( int & bucket_id,
bool fw )

Computes the phi values for a given bucket ID and direction.

Definition at line 141 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

141 {
142 std::vector<int> phi;
143
144 // Ensure bucket_id is within valid bounds
145 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
146 auto &fixed_buckets = fw ? fw_fixed_buckets : bw_fixed_buckets;
147 auto &node_interval_trees = fw ? fw_node_interval_trees : bw_node_interval_trees;
148
149 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
150 if (bucket_id >= buckets.size() || bucket_id < 0) return phi;
151
152 std::vector<double> total_ranges(intervals.size());
153 std::vector<double> base_intervals(intervals.size());
154
155 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
156 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r]; // Ensure integer type for total range
157 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
158 }
159
160 // Get the node ID and current bucket
161 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
162 auto &current_bucket = buckets[bucket_id];
163
164 // Retrieve the pre-built Splay Tree for this node
165 auto &node_tree = node_interval_trees[node_id];
166
167 // Search for matching intervals using the existing Splay Tree
168 if (fw) {
169 // Forward search: find the interval just below the current bucket
170 std::vector<double> target_low = current_bucket.lb;
171 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
172 target_low[r] -= base_intervals[r]; // Adjust for the base intervals
173 }
174
175 TreeNode *found_node = node_tree.find(target_low);
176 if (found_node != nullptr && buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
177 // Check if the found bucket is fixed
178#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
179 if (fixed_buckets[found_node->bucket_index][bucket_id] == 0)
180#endif
181 {
182 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
183 }
184 }
185 } else {
186 // Backward search: find the interval just above the current bucket
187 std::vector<double> target_high = current_bucket.ub;
188 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
189 target_high[r] += base_intervals[r]; // Adjust for the base intervals
190 }
191
192 TreeNode *found_node = node_tree.find(target_high);
193 if (found_node != nullptr && buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
194 // Check if the found bucket is fixed
195#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
196 if (fixed_buckets[found_node->bucket_index][bucket_id] == 0)
197#endif
198 {
199 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
200 }
201 }
202 }
203
204 } else {
205 // Handle the case where R_SIZE == 1 with a simpler approach
206 int smaller = bucket_id - 1;
207
208 if (smaller >= 0 && buckets[smaller].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
209#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
210 if (fixed_buckets[smaller][bucket_id] == 0)
211#endif
212 {
213 phi.push_back(smaller);
214 }
215 }
216 }
217 return phi;
218}

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::ConcatenateLabel ( const Label * L,
int & b,
double & best_cost,
std::vector< uint64_t > & Bvisited )

Concatenates the label L with the bucket b and updates the best label pbest.

446 {
447 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
448 bucket_stack.clear();
449 bucket_stack.reserve(50);
450 bucket_stack.push_back(b);
451
452 auto &other_buckets = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
453 auto &other_c_bar = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
454
455 // Cache frequently accessed values
456 const int L_node_id = L->node_id;
457 const auto &L_resources = L->resources;
458 const auto &L_last_node = nodes[L_node_id];
459 const double L_cost = L->cost;
460 const size_t bitmap_size = L->visited_bitmap.size();
461
462 // Pre-compute constants for bit operations
463 constexpr uint64_t one = 1ULL;
464 const bool has_branching = !branching_duals->empty();
465
466 // SRC mode setup
467#if defined(SRC)
468 decltype(cut_storage) cutter = nullptr;
469 decltype(cut_storage->SRCDuals) *SRCDuals = nullptr;
470 if constexpr (S > Stage::Three) {
471 cutter = cut_storage;
472 SRCDuals = &cutter->SRCDuals;
473 }
474#endif
475
476 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
477 const int current_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
478 bucket_stack.pop_back();
479
480 // Optimize bit operations for visited tracking
481 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
482 const uint64_t bit_mask = one << (current_bucket & 63);
483 Bvisited[segment] |= bit_mask;
484
485 const int bucketLprimenode = other_buckets[current_bucket].node_id;
486 double travel_cost = getcij(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
487
488 // Apply arc duals if needed
489#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
490 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) { travel_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode); }
491#endif
492
493 if (has_branching) { travel_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode); }
494
495 const double path_cost = L_cost + travel_cost;
496 const double bound = other_c_bar[current_bucket];
497
498 // Early bound check
499 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= best_cost) ||
500 (S == Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= gap)) {
501 continue;
502 }
503
504 // Process labels in current bucket
505 const auto &bucket = other_buckets[current_bucket];
506 const auto &labels = bucket.get_labels();
507
508 for (const Label *L_bw : labels) {
509 // Early rejection tests
510 if (L_bw->node_id == L_node_id || !check_feasibility(L, L_bw)) { continue; }
511
512 // Visited nodes overlap check
513 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three) {
514 bool has_overlap = false;
515 for (size_t i = 0; i < bitmap_size; ++i) {
516 if (L->visited_bitmap[i] & L_bw->visited_bitmap[i]) {
517 has_overlap = true;
518 break;
519 }
520 }
521 if (has_overlap) continue;
522 }
523
524 double total_cost = path_cost + L_bw->cost;
525
526 // SRC cost adjustments
527#if defined(SRC)
528 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
529 for (auto it = cutter->begin(); it < cutter->end(); ++it) {
530 const auto &dual = (*SRCDuals)[it->id];
531 if (dual == 0) continue;
532
533 if (L->SRCmap[it->id] + L_bw->SRCmap[it->id] >= it->p.den) { total_cost -= dual; }
534 }
535 }
536#endif
537
538 // Cost-based filtering
539 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= best_cost) || (S == Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= gap)) {
540 continue;
541 }
542
543 // Create new merged label
544 auto pbest = compute_label<S>(L, L_bw);
545 best_cost = pbest->cost;
546 // fmt::print("Best cost: {}\n", best_cost);
547 merged_labels.push_back(pbest);
548 }
549
550 // Process neighbor buckets
551 for (int b_prime : Phi_bw[current_bucket]) {
552 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
553 const uint64_t mask_prime = one << (b_prime & 63);
554 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask_prime)) { bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime); }
555 }
556 }
557}

constexpr auto & assign_symmetry(auto &FW, auto &BW) noexcept

◆ define_buckets()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

82 {
83 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
84 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
85 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
86
87 // Ensure the base_intervals storage is resized for all nodes
88 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
89 fw_base_intervals.resize(num_intervals);
90 } else {
91 bw_base_intervals.resize(num_intervals);
92 }
93
94 // Determine the base interval and other relevant values for each resource
95 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
96 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
97 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
98 }
99
100 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
101 fw_base_intervals = base_intervals;
102 } else {
103 bw_base_intervals = base_intervals;
104 }
105
106 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
107 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
108 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
109 auto &node_interval_trees = assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
110 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
111 num_buckets.resize(nodes.size());
112 num_buckets_index.resize(nodes.size());
113
114 int cum_sum = 0; // Tracks global bucket index
115 int bucket_index = 0;
116
117 node_interval_trees.assign(nodes.size(), SplayTree());
118
119 // Loop through each node to define its specific buckets
120 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
121 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
122 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
123 node_base_interval[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
124 }
125 SplayTree node_tree;
126
127 // Multiple buckets case
128 int n_buckets = 0;
129 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
130 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals), interval_end(num_intervals);
131
132 // Calculate the start and end for each interval dimension
133 // Check if there is only one interval and proceed with the single interval logic
134 if (num_intervals == 1) {
135 for (auto j = 0; j < intervals[0].interval; ++j) {
136 // Perform the same interval logic for a single interval
137 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
138 interval_start[0] = VRPNode.lb[0] + current_pos[0] * node_base_interval[0];
139
140 if (j == intervals[0].interval - 1) {
141 interval_end[0] = VRPNode.ub[0];
142 } else {
143 interval_end[0] = VRPNode.lb[0] + (current_pos[0] + 1) * node_base_interval[0];
144 }
145 } else {
146 if (j == intervals[0].interval - 1) {
147 interval_start[0] = VRPNode.lb[0];
148 } else {
149 interval_start[0] = VRPNode.ub[0] - (current_pos[0] + 1) * node_base_interval[0];
150 }
151 interval_end[0] = VRPNode.ub[0] - current_pos[0] * node_base_interval[0];
152 }
153
154 interval_start[0] = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(interval_start[0]);
155 interval_end[0] = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(interval_end[0]);
156
157 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
158 interval_start[0] = std::max(interval_start[0], VRPNode.lb[0]);
159 } else {
160 interval_end[0] = std::min(interval_end[0], VRPNode.ub[0]);
161 }
162 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
163 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index);
164
165 bucket_index++;
166 n_buckets++;
167 cum_sum++;
168 current_pos[0]++;
169 }
170 } else {
171 // Multiple intervals case, nested loop logic to generate all combinations
172 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
173
174 while (true) {
175 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
176 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
177 interval_start[r] = VRPNode.lb[r] + pos[r] * node_base_interval[r];
178
179 if (pos[r] == intervals[r].interval - 1) {
180 interval_end[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
181 } else {
182 interval_end[r] = VRPNode.lb[r] + (pos[r] + 1) * node_base_interval[r];
183 }
184 } else {
185 if (pos[r] == intervals[r].interval - 1) {
186 interval_start[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
187 } else {
188 interval_start[r] = VRPNode.ub[r] - (pos[r] + 1) * node_base_interval[r];
189 }
190 interval_end[r] = VRPNode.ub[r] - pos[r] * node_base_interval[r];
191 }
192
193 interval_start[r] = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(interval_start[r]);
194 interval_end[r] = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(interval_end[r]);
195
196 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
197 interval_start[r] = std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
198 } else {
199 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
200 }
201 }
202
203 /*
204 // Print and store
205 fmt::print("Creating bucket with interval: [");
206 for (int i = 0; i < num_intervals; ++i) {
207 fmt::print("({}, {})", interval_start[i], interval_end[i]);
208 if (i < num_intervals - 1) fmt::print(", ");
209 }
210 fmt::print("]\n");
211 */
212
213 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
214 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index);
215
216 bucket_index++;
217 n_buckets++;
218 cum_sum++;
219
220 // Increment the positions for combinations
221 int i = 0;
222 while (i < num_intervals && ++pos[i] >= intervals[i].interval) {
223 pos[i] = 0;
224 i++;
225 }
226 if (i == num_intervals) break; // All combinations generated
227 }
228 }
229
230 // Update node-specific bucket data
231 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
232 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
233 node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
234 }
235
236 // Update global bucket sizes based on direction
237 buckets_size = cum_sum;
238}

281 {
282 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
283
284 // Initialize elementarity sets
285 elementarity_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for elementarity sets
286
287 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
288 // Each customer should appear in its own elementarity set
289 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
290 elementarity_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
291
292 // Mark the node itself in the elementarity set
293 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
294 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
295 elementarity_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
296 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > &__restrict__ bucket_order )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

Definition at line 956 of file BucketSolve.h.

961 // Use references to avoid indirection
962 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
963 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
964 auto &uf = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_find, bw_union_find);
965
966 // Cache frequently used values
967 const int b_L = L->vertex;
968 const double label_cost = L->cost;
969 const size_t res_size = options.resources.size();
970
971 // Pre-compute bit operation constants
972 constexpr uint64_t one = 1ULL;
973 constexpr uint64_t bit_mask_lookup[64] = {
974 1ULL << 0, 1ULL << 1, /* ... pre-compute all 64 values ... */
975 };
976
977 // Stack-based implementation with fixed size for small bucket counts
978 constexpr size_t MAX_STACK_SIZE = 128;
979 int stack_buffer[MAX_STACK_SIZE];
980 int stack_size = 1;
981 stack_buffer[0] = bucket;
982
983 // SIMD-optimized dominance check function
984 auto check_dominance = [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels) {
985#ifdef __AVX2__
986 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(L, labels, cut_storage,
987 res_size);
988#else
989 const size_t n = labels.size();
990 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
991 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i])) return true;
992 }
993 return false;
994#endif
995 };
996
997 do {
998 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer[--stack_size];
999
1000 // Optimize bit operations
1001 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
1002 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
1003 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
1004
1005 // Early exit check
1006 if (__builtin_expect(
1007 (label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] &&
1008 ::precedes<int>(bucket_order, current_bucket, b_L, uf)),
1009 0)) {
1010 return false;
1011 }
1012
1013 // Check dominance only if necessary
1014 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1015 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1016 int stat_n_dom = 0;
1017 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance(L, check_dominance, stat_n_dom)) {
1018 return true;
1019 }
1020 }
1021
1022 // Process neighbors using SIMD if available
1023 const auto &bucket_neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1024 const size_t n_neighbors = bucket_neighbors.size();
1025
1026 // Regular processing
1027 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_neighbors; ++i) {
1028 const int b_prime = bucket_neighbors[i];
1029 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
1030 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63])) {
1031 if (stack_size < MAX_STACK_SIZE) {
1032 stack_buffer[stack_size++] = b_prime;
1033 } else {
1034 fmt::print(
1035 "Stack overflow in "
1036 "DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets\n");
1037 return false;
1038 }
1039 }
1040 }
1041
1042 } while (stack_size > 0);
1043
1044 return false;
1045}

template<Direction D, Stage S, ArcType A, Mutability M, Full F>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::Extend ( const std::conditional_t< M==Mutability::Mut, Label *, const Label * > L_prime,
const std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Bucket, BucketArc, std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Jump, JumpArc, Arc > > & gamma,
int depth )
inlinenoexcept

Extends the label L_prime with the given BucketArc gamma.

581 {
582 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
583 new_resources.resize(options.resources.size());
584
585 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
586 auto initial_resources = L_prime->resources; // Make a copy as in original
587 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
588
589 int node_id;
590 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
591 node_id =
592 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
593 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
594 node_id = gamma.to;
595 } else {
596 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
597 node_id = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket].node_id;
598
599 // Update copy of resources for jump arcs
600 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
601 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
602 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
603 initial_resources[i] = std::max(
604 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
605 } else {
606 initial_resources[i] = std::min(
607 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
608 }
609 }
610 }
611
612 // Early rejection checks
613 if (node_id == L_prime->node_id ||
614 is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
615 return {};
616 }
617
618 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
619 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
620 fixed_arcs[initial_node_id][node_id] == 1) ||
621 (D == Direction::Backward &&
622 fixed_arcs[node_id][initial_node_id] == 1)) {
623 return {};
624 }
625 }
626
627 // Process resources and check feasibility
628 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
629 if (!process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources, gamma,
630 nodes[node_id],
631 options.resources.size())) {
632 return {};
633 }
634 }
635
636 // Path size checks for PSTEP/TSP
637 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
638 int n_visited = 0;
639 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap) {
640 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
641 }
642
643 if (n_visited > options.max_path_size ||
644 (n_visited == options.max_path_size &&
645 node_id != options.end_depot)) {
646 return {};
647 }
648 }
649
650 const int to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
651
652 // Handle fixed buckets
653#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
654 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four && A != ArcType::Jump) {
655 auto &fixed_buckets =
656 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
657 if (fixed_buckets[L_prime->vertex][to_bucket] == 1) {
658 if (depth > 1) return {};
659
660 static thread_local std::vector<Label *>
661 label_vector; // Reuse vector
662 label_vector.clear();
663
664 for (const auto &jump_arc :
665 nodes[L_prime->node_id].template get_jump_arcs<D>(node_id)) {
666 auto extended_labels =
667 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Jump, Mutability::Const, F>(
668 L_prime, jump_arc, depth + 1);
669 if (!extended_labels.empty()) {
670 label_vector.insert(label_vector.end(),
671 extended_labels.begin(),
672 extended_labels.end());
673 }
674 }
675 return label_vector;
676 }
677 }
678#endif
679
680 // Cost computation
681 double new_cost = initial_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
682 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
683
684 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
685 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
686 } else {
687 int n_visited = 0;
688 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap) {
689 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
690 }
691
692 if (n_visited > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot) {
693 new_cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id) +
694 pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
695 }
696 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) +
697 pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
698 }
699
700 // Branching duals
701 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
702 new_cost += (D == Direction::Forward)
703 ? branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
704 : branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
705 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
706 }
707
708 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
709 new_cost -= (D == Direction::Forward)
710 ? arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
711 : arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
712 })
713
714 // Handle Reverse case
715 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
716 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <=
717 q_star[options.main_resources[0]]) {
718 return {};
719 }
720 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
721 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
722 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
723 return {new_label};
724 }
725
726 // Create and initialize new label
727 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
728 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
729 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
730 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
731 new_label->real_cost =
732 L_prime->real_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
733
734 if constexpr (M == Mutability::Mut) {
735 new_label->parent = L_prime;
736 }
737
738 // Handle visited bitmap
739 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
740 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
741 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
742 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
743 if (current_visited) {
744 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
745 current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i];
746 }
747 }
748 }
749 } else {
750 new_label->visited_bitmap = L_prime->visited_bitmap;
751 }
752 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
753
754 // new_label->nodes_covered = L_prime->nodes_covered; // Copy the list of
755 // covered nodes new_label->nodes_covered.push_back(node_id); //
756 // Add the new node to the list of covered nodes
757
758#if defined(SRC)
759 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
760 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
761 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
762 std::span<const double> SRCDuals{cutter->SRCDuals};
763 const size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
764 const size_t bit_position = node_id & 63;
765 const uint64_t bit_mask = 1ULL << bit_position;
766 const size_t cut_size = cutter->size();
767
768 static constexpr size_t SMALL_SIZE = 256;
769
770#if defined(__cpp_lib_ranges)
771 auto indices = std::views::iota(size_t{0}, cut_size);
772#else
773 static thread_local std::vector<size_t> indices;
774 indices.resize(cut_size);
775 std::iota(indices.begin(), indices.end(), 0);
776#endif
777
778 auto process_cut = [&](size_t idx) -> double {
779 if (SRCDuals[idx] > -1e-3) {
780 return 0.0;
781 }
782
783 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
784 if (!(cut.neighbors[segment] & bit_mask)) {
785 new_label->SRCmap[idx] = 0.0;
786 return 0.0;
787 }
788
789 if (cut.baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
790 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
791 const auto &den = multipliers.den;
792 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[idx];
793 src_map_value += multipliers.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
794 if (src_map_value >= den) {
795 src_map_value -= den;
796 return -SRCDuals[idx];
797 }
798 }
799 return 0.0;
800 };
801
802 double total_cost_update;
803 if (cut_size <= SMALL_SIZE) {
804 total_cost_update = 0.0;
805 for (size_t i = 0; i < cut_size; ++i) {
806 total_cost_update += process_cut(i);
807 }
808 } else {
809#if defined(__cpp_lib_parallel_algorithm)
810 total_cost_update = std::transform_reduce(
811 std::execution::par_unseq, std::begin(indices),
812 std::end(indices), 0.0, std::plus<>(), process_cut);
813#else
814 total_cost_update =
815 std::transform_reduce(std::begin(indices), std::end(indices),
816 0.0, std::plus<>(), process_cut);
817#endif
818 }
819
820 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
821 }
822#endif
823
824 // Return the array (as Label**)
825 return std::vector<Label *>{new_label};
826}
RCC_MODE_BLOCK
#define RCC_MODE_BLOCK(code_block)

◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 344 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

344 {
345 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
346 int v1 = cycle[i];
347 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
348
349 // Update the bitmap to forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1
350 size_t segment = v2 >> 6;
351 size_t bit_position = v2 & 63;
352 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
353
354 if (aggressive) {
355 segment = v1 >> 6;
356 bit_position = v1 & 63;
357 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
358 }
359 }
360}

◆ generate_arcs() [1/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 831 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

831 {
832
833 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
834 work, bi_sched,
835 SECTION {
836 // Task for Forward Direction
837 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
838 Phi_fw.clear();
839 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
840 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) { Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true); }
841 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
842 },
843 SECTION {
844 // Task for Backward Direction
845 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
846 Phi_bw.clear();
847 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
848 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) { Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false); }
849 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
850 });
851}

◆ generate_arcs() [2/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Definition at line 247 of file BucketUtils.h.

247 {
248 // Mutex to synchronize access to shared resources
249 auto buckets_mutex = std::mutex();
250
251 // Clear the appropriate bucket graph (forward or backward)
252 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
253 fw_bucket_graph.clear(); // Clear the forward bucket graph
254 } else {
255 bw_bucket_graph.clear(); // Clear the backward bucket graph
256 }
257
258 // Assign the forward or backward fixed buckets and other bucket-related structures
259 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
260 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
261 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
262 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
263
264 // Clear all buckets in parallel, removing any existing arcs
265 std::for_each(buckets.begin(), buckets.end(), [&](auto &bucket) {
266 bucket.clear(); // Clear bucket data
267 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward); // Clear arcs in the bucket
268 });
269
270 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket, std::vector<double> &res_inc,
271 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> &local_arcs) {
272 // Retrieve the arcs for the node in the given direction (Forward/Backward)
273 auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
274 // Compute base intervals for each resource dimension based on R_max and R_min
275 std::vector<double> node_intervals(options.resources.size());
276 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
277 node_intervals[r] = (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
278 }
279
280 // Iterate over all arcs of the node
281 for (const auto &arc : arcs) {
282 const auto &next_node = nodes[arc.to]; // Get the destination node of the arc
283
284 std::vector<double> next_node_intervals(options.resources.size());
285 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
286 next_node_intervals[r] = (next_node.ub[r] - next_node.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
287 }
288 // Skip self-loops (no arc from a node to itself)
289 if (node.id == next_node.id) continue;
290
291 // Calculate travel cost and cost increment based on node's properties
292 const auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
293 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
294 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); r++) {
295 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
296 if (options.resources[r] == "time") { res_inc[r] += travel_cost; }
297 }
298
299 for (int j = 0; j < num_buckets[next_node.id]; ++j) {
300 int to_bucket = j + num_buckets_index[next_node.id];
301 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
302
303 if (fixed_buckets[from_bucket][to_bucket] == 1) continue; // Skip fixed arcs
304
305 bool valid_arc = true;
306 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size(); ++r) {
307 // Forward direction: Check that resource increment doesn't exceed upper bounds
308 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
309 if (buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
310 valid_arc = false;
311 break;
312 }
313 }
314 // Backward direction: Check that resource decrement doesn't drop below lower bounds
315 else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
316 if (buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r] < next_node.lb[r]) {
317 valid_arc = false;
318 break;
319 }
320 }
321 }
322 if (!valid_arc) continue; // Skip invalid arcs
323
324 // Further refine arc validity based on the base intervals and node bounds
325 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
326 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size(); ++r) {
327 double max_calc = std::max(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.lb[r]);
328 if (max_calc < buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] ||
329 max_calc >= buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] + next_node_intervals[r] + numericutils::eps) {
330 valid_arc = false;
331 break;
332 }
333 }
334 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
335 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size(); ++r) {
336 double min_calc = std::min(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r]);
337 if (min_calc > buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] ||
338 min_calc <= buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] - next_node_intervals[r] - numericutils::eps) {
339 valid_arc = false;
340 break;
341 }
342 }
343 }
344 if (!valid_arc) continue; // Skip invalid arcs
345
346 // Store the arc data locally before committing it to the global structure
347 local_arcs.emplace_back(from_bucket, to_bucket);
348 double local_cost_inc = cost_inc;
349 std::vector<double> local_res_inc = res_inc;
350
351 // Add the arc to the global structure and the bucket
352 {
353 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(buckets_mutex); // Lock the mutex to ensure thread safety
354 add_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, local_res_inc, local_cost_inc); // Add the arc globally
355 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, local_res_inc,
356 local_cost_inc, false); // Add the arc to the bucket
357 }
358 }
359 }
360 };
361
362 unsigned int total_threads = std::thread::hardware_concurrency();
363 // Divide by 2 to use only half of the available threads
364 unsigned int half_threads = total_threads / 2;
365
366 const int JOBS = half_threads;
367 exec::static_thread_pool pool(JOBS);
368 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
369
370 // Iterate over all nodes in parallel, generating arcs for each
371 std::vector<int> tasks; // Tasks will store node ids
372 for (int node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
373 tasks.push_back(node_id); // Store the node id to be processed
374 }
375
376 // Define chunk size to reduce parallelization overhead
377 const int chunk_size = 10; // Adjust based on your performance needs
378
379 // Parallel execution in chunks
380 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
381 stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
382 [this, &tasks, &num_buckets, &num_buckets_index, &add_arcs_for_node, chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
383 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
384 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
385
386 // Process a chunk of tasks (i.e., a group of nodes)
387 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
388 int node_id = tasks[task_idx]; // Get the node id
389 const VRPNode &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
390
391 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size()); // Resource increment vector
392 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> local_arcs; // Local storage for arcs
393
394 // Generate arcs for all buckets associated with the current node
395 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[VRPNode.id]; ++i) {
396 int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id]; // Determine the source bucket
397 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, from_bucket, res_inc,
398 local_arcs); // Add arcs for this node and bucket
399 }
400 }
401 });
402
403 // Submit work to the thread pool
404 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
405 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
406}

Represents a bucket in the Bucket Graph. Adds an arc to the bucket graph.

◆ get_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
AdjList BucketGraph::get_adjacency_list ( )
inline

Definition at line 1080 of file BucketGraph.h.

1080 {
1081 AdjList adjacency_list;
1082
1083 // Iterate through all nodes in the graph
1084 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1085 std::vector<std::tuple<int, double, int>> arcs;
1086
1087 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1088 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {
1089 int to_node = arc.to;
1090 double cost = getcij(node.id, to_node) - nodes[to_node].cost;
1091 int capacity = 1;
1092
1093 // Only add arcs with non-zero capacity
1094 if (capacity > 0) { arcs.emplace_back(to_node, cost, capacity); }
1095 }
1096
1097 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has any outgoing edges
1098 if (!arcs.empty()) { adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs; }
1099 }
1100
1101 return adjacency_list;
1102 }

◆ get_best_label()

template<Direction D>
Label * BucketGraph::get_best_label ( const std::vector< int > & topological_order,
const std::vector< double > & c_bar,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > & sccs )

Retrieves the best label from the bucket graph based on the given topological order, c_bar values, and strongly connected components.

This function iterates through the given topological order and for each component, it retrieves the labels from the corresponding buckets in the bucket graph. It then compares the cost of each label and keeps track of the label with the lowest cost. The best label, along with its associated bucket, is returned.

Definition at line 418 of file BucketUtils.h.

419 {
420 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
421 Label *best_label = nullptr; // Ensure this is initialized
422 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
423
424 for (const int component_index : topological_order) {
425 const auto &component_buckets = sccs[component_index];
426
427 for (const int bucket : component_buckets) {
428 const auto &label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label();
429 if (!label) continue;
430 // print label->cost
431 if (label->cost < best_cost) {
432 best_cost = label->cost;
433 best_label = label;
434 }
435 }
436 }
437
438 return best_label;
439}

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 738 of file BucketUtils.h.

738 {
739
740 // TODO: adjust to multi-resource case
741 auto &current_bucket =
742 (D == Direction::Forward) ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index] : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
743 int &node = current_bucket.node_id;
744 auto &theNode = nodes[node];
745
746 // Find the opposite bucket using the appropriate direction
747 int opposite_bucket_index = -1;
748 std::vector<double> reference_point(options.main_resources.size());
749 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
750 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
751 reference_point[r] = std::max(inc[r], theNode.lb[r]);
752 } else {
753 reference_point[r] = std::min(inc[r], theNode.ub[r]);
754 }
755 }
756 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
757 opposite_bucket_index = get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
758 } else {
759 opposite_bucket_index = get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
760 }
761
762 return opposite_bucket_index;
763}

◆ heuristic_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

Definition at line 833 of file BucketUtils.h.

833 {
834 // Stage 3 fixing heuristic
835 reset_pool();
836 reset_fixed();
837 common_initialization();
838
839 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
840 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
841
842 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Two, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
843
844 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> fw_labels_map(nodes.size());
845 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> bw_labels_map(nodes.size());
846
847 auto group_labels = [&](auto &buckets, auto &labels_map) {
848 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
849 for (auto label : bucket.get_labels()) {
850 labels_map[label->node_id].push_back(label); // Directly index using node_id
851 }
852 }
853 };
854
855 // Create tasks for forward and backward labels grouping
856 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() { group_labels(fw_buckets, fw_labels_map); });
857 auto backward_task =
858 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() { group_labels(bw_buckets, bw_labels_map); });
859
860 // Execute the tasks in parallel
861 auto work = stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task));
862
863 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
864
865 auto num_fixes = 0;
866 // Function to find the minimum cost label in a vector of labels
867 auto find_min_cost_label = [](const std::vector<Label *> &labels) -> const Label * {
868 return *std::min_element(labels.begin(), labels.end(),
869 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
870 };
871 for (const auto &node_I : nodes) {
872 const auto &fw_labels = fw_labels_map[node_I.id];
873 if (fw_labels.empty()) continue; // Skip if no labels for this node_id
874
875 for (const auto &node_J : nodes) {
876 if (node_I.id == node_J.id) continue; // Compare based on id (or other key field)
877 const auto &bw_labels = bw_labels_map[node_J.id];
878 if (bw_labels.empty()) continue; // Skip if no labels for this node_id
879
880 const Label *min_fw_label = find_min_cost_label(fw_labels);
881 const Label *min_bw_label = find_min_cost_label(bw_labels);
882
883 if (!min_fw_label || !min_bw_label) continue;
884
885 const VRPNode &L_last_node = nodes[min_fw_label->node_id];
886 auto cost = getcij(min_fw_label->node_id, min_bw_label->node_id);
887
888 bool violated = false;
889 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
890 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
891 if (min_fw_label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + L_last_node.duration >
892 min_bw_label->resources[TIME_INDEX]) {
893 violated = true;
894 break;
895 }
896 } else {
897 if (min_fw_label->resources[r] + L_last_node.consumption[r] > min_bw_label->resources[r]) {
898 violated = true;
899 break;
900 }
901 }
902 }
903
904 if (violated) continue;
905
906 if (min_fw_label->cost + cost + min_bw_label->cost > gap) {
907 fixed_arcs[node_I.id][node_J.id] = 1; // Index with node ids
908 num_fixes++;
909 }
910 }
911 }
912}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 915 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

915 {
916
917 // Print header
918 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
919 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
920 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
921
922 // Print Resource size
923 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
924 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
925
926 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
927#ifdef RIH
928 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
929#else
930 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
931#endif
932#ifdef RCC
933 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
934#else
935 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
936#endif
937#ifdef SRC
938 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
939#else
940 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
941#endif
942 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
943
944 fmt::print("\n");
945}

◆ is_dominated()

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::is_dominated ( const Label * new_label,
const Label * labels )
inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

Definition at line 834 of file BucketSolve.h.

835 {
836 // Extract resources for the new label and the comparison label
837 const auto &new_resources = new_label->resources;
838 const auto &label_resources = label->resources;
839
840 {
841 // Simple cost check: if the comparison label has a higher cost, it is
842 // not dominated
843 if (label->cost > new_label->cost) {
844 return false;
845 }
846 }
847
848 // Check resource conditions based on the direction (Forward or Backward)
849 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
850 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
851 // In Forward direction: the comparison label must not have more
852 // resources than the new label
853 if (label_resources[i] > new_resources[i]) {
854 return false;
855 }
856 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
857 // In Backward direction: the comparison label must not have fewer
858 // resources than the new label
859 if (label_resources[i] < new_resources[i]) {
860 return false;
861 }
862 }
863 }
864
865 // TODO:: check again this unreachable dominance
866#ifndef UNREACHABLE_DOMINANCE
867 // Check visited nodes (bitmap comparison) for Stages 3, 4, and Enumerate
868 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
869 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
870 // Iterate through the visited bitmap and ensure that the new label
871 // visits all nodes that the comparison label visits
872 for (size_t i = 0; i < label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
873 // If the comparison label visits a node that the new label does
874 // not, it is not dominated
875 if (((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) ^
876 label->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
877 return false;
878 }
879 }
880 }
881#else
882 // Unreachable dominance logic: check visited and unreachable nodes in
883 // Stages 3, 4, and Enumerate
884 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
885 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
886 for (size_t i = 0; i < label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
887 // Combine visited and unreachable nodes in the comparison label's
888 // bitmap
889 auto combined_label_bitmap =
890 label->visited_bitmap[i] | label->unreachable_bitmap[i];
891 // Ensure the new label visits all nodes that the comparison label
892 // (or its unreachable nodes) visits
893 if ((combined_label_bitmap & ~new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) {
894 return false;
895 }
896 }
897 }
898#endif
899
900#ifdef SRC
901 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
902 double sumSRC = 0;
903
904 const auto &SRCDuals = cut_storage->SRCDuals;
905 if (!SRCDuals.empty()) {
906 const auto &labelSRCMap = label->SRCmap;
907 const auto &newLabelSRCMap = new_label->SRCmap;
908 for (size_t i = 0; i < SRCDuals.size(); ++i) {
909 const auto &den = cut_storage->getCut(i).p.den;
910 const auto labelMod = labelSRCMap[i]; // % den;
911 const auto newLabelMod = newLabelSRCMap[i]; // % den;
912 if (labelMod > newLabelMod) {
913 sumSRC += SRCDuals[i];
914 }
915 }
916 }
917 if (label->cost - sumSRC > new_label->cost) {
918 return false;
919 }
920 }
921#endif
922
923 // If all conditions are met, return true, indicating that the new label is
924 // dominated by the comparison label
925 return true;
926}

bool BucketGraph::is_in_neighborhood ( size_t i,

Definition at line 912 of file BucketGraph.h.

912 {
913 if (i >= neighborhoods_bitmap.size()) return false;
914
915 const size_t segment = j >> 6;
916 const size_t bit_position = j & 63;
917
918 if (segment >= neighborhoods_bitmap[i].size()) return false;
919
920 return (neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] & (1ULL << bit_position)) != 0;
921 }

◆ is_node_unreachable()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

Definition at line 473 of file BucketGraph.h.

473 {
474 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment contains the node_id
475 int bit_position = node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment
476 return (bitmap[word_index] & (1ULL << bit_position)) != 0;
477 }

◆ is_node_visited()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

446 {

450 }

std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

218 // Use references to avoid repeated lookups

221 auto &topological_order =
222 assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
223 auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
224 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
