226 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
227 auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
228 auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
229 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
230 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
231
232 n_labels = 0;
233
234 // Pre-calculate segment info
235 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) / 64;
236 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments);
237
238 // Preallocate vector for new labels to avoid repeated allocations
239 std::vector<Label *> new_labels;
240 new_labels.reserve(16); // Adjust size based on typical usage
241
242 // Process SCCs in topological order
243 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
244 bool all_ext;
245 do {
246 all_ext = true;
247
248 // Process buckets in sorted order within SCC
249 for (const auto bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
250 // Cache bucket labels reference
251 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_labels();
252 const size_t n_bucket_labels = bucket_labels.size();
253
254 for (size_t label_idx = 0; label_idx < n_bucket_labels;
255 ++label_idx) {
256 Label *label = bucket_labels[label_idx];
257 if (label->is_extended) continue;
258
259 // Early resource check for partial solutions
260 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
261 if constexpr (F == Full::Partial) {
262 const double main_resource =
263 label->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
264 const double q_star_value =
265 q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
266
267 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
268 if (main_resource > q_star_value) {
269 label->set_extended(true);
270 continue;
271 }
272 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
273 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) {
274 label->set_extended(true);
275 continue;
276 }
277 }
278 }
279 }
280
281 // Efficient visited bucket clearing
282 if (n_segments <= 8) {
283 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_segments; ++i) {
284 Bvisited[i] = 0;
285 }
286 } else {
287 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
288 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
289 }
290
291 // Check dominance in smaller buckets
292 bool domin_smaller = false;
293 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
294 domin_smaller = DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(
295 label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, ordered_sccs);
296 }
297
298 if (!domin_smaller) {
299 // Process arcs for current label
300 const auto &node_arcs =
301 nodes[label->node_id].get_arcs<D>(scc_index);
302
303 for (const auto &arc : node_arcs) {
304 new_labels.clear(); // Reuse vector
305 auto extended_labels =
306 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Node, Mutability::Mut, F>(
307 label, arc);
308
309 if (extended_labels.empty()) {
310#ifdef UNREACHABLE_DOMINANCE
311 set_node_unreachable(label->unreachable_bitmap,
312 arc.to);
313#endif
314 continue;
315 }
316
317 // Process each new label
318 for (Label *new_label : extended_labels) {
319 stat_n_labels++;
320
321 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
322 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
323 const auto &to_bucket_labels =
324 mother_bucket.get_labels();
325
326 // Stage-specific dominance checking
327 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP &&
328 F != Full::TSP) {
329 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four &&
330 D == Direction::Forward) {
331 dominance_checks_per_bucket
332 [to_bucket] +=
333 to_bucket_labels.size();
334 }
335 }
336
337 bool dominated = false;
338 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
339 // Optimized Stage One processing
340 for (auto *existing_label :
341 to_bucket_labels) {
342 if (label->cost <
343 existing_label->cost) {
344 mother_bucket.remove_label(
345 existing_label);
346 } else {
347 dominated = true;
348 break;
349 }
350 }
351 } else {
352 // Use existing SIMD implementation for
353 // other stages
354 auto check_dominance_in_bucket =
355 [&](const std::vector<Label *>
356 &labels) {
357#ifndef __AVX2__
358 for (auto *existing_label :
359 labels) {
360 if (is_dominated<D, S>(
361 new_label,
362 existing_label)) {
363 stat_n_dom++;
364 return true;
365 }
366 }
367 return false;
368#else
369 return check_dominance_against_vector<
370 D, S>(new_label, labels,
371 cut_storage,
372 options.resources.size());
373#endif
374 };
375
376 dominated = mother_bucket.check_dominance(
377 new_label, check_dominance_in_bucket,
378 stat_n_dom);
379 }
380
381 if (!dominated) {
382 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
383 for (auto *existing_label :
384 to_bucket_labels) {
385 if (is_dominated<D, S>(
386 existing_label,
387 new_label)) {
388 mother_bucket.remove_label(
389 existing_label);
390 }
391 }
392 }
393
394 n_labels++;
395#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
396 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
397#elif LIMITED_BUCKETS
398 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label,
399 BUCKET_CAPACITY);
400#else
401 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
402#endif
403 all_ext = false;
404 }
405 }
406 }
407 }
408
409 label->set_extended(true);
410 }
411 }
412
413 // Update c_bar values efficiently
414 for (const int bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
415 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
416 for (const auto phi_bucket : Phi[bucket]) {
417 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_bucket]);
418 }
419 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
420 }
421 } while (!all_ext);
422 }
423
424 // Store best label
425 Label *best_label = get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs);
426 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
427 fw_best_label = best_label;
428 } else {
429 bw_best_label = best_label;
430 }
431
432 return c_bar;
433}

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

Definition at line 658 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

658 {
659 // Clear previous data
660 merged_labels.clear();
661 merged_labels.reserve(100);
662 fw_c_bar.clear();
663 bw_c_bar.clear();
664
665 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
666 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
667
668 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
669 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
670 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
671
672 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
673 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
674
675 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
676 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
677 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
678
679 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
680
681 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
682 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
683 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
684 }
685
686 // Clear forward and backward buckets
687 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
688 fw_buckets[b].clear();
689 bw_buckets[b].clear();
690 }
691
692 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
693 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
694
695 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
696 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
697
698 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper bounds
699 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
700 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
701 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
702 }
703 int offset_fw = 0;
704 int offset_bw = 0;
705
706 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across combinations
707 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
708 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
709 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
710 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
711 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
712 // print depot_id
713 int calculated_index_base =
714 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot] : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
715
716 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
717 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
718
719 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
720 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
721 if (depth == num_intervals) {
722 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current combination
723 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
724 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
725
726 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval starts or ends
727 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
728 interval_bounds[r] =
729 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] + current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
730 : interval_ends[r] - current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r]);
731 }
732
733 // print interval
734 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
735 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds, depot_id);
736 depot->is_extended = false;
737 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
738 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
739 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
740 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
741 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
742 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
743
744 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
745 return;
746 }
747
748 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
749 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
750 current_pos[depth] = k;
751 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
752 }
753 };
754
755 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
756 generate_combinations(0);
757 };
758
759 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring all combinations are processed
760 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
761}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 265 of file BucketJump.h.

265 {
266 // Assign forward or backward buckets, fixed buckets, bucket indices, and Phi (adjacency list) based on
267 // direction
268 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
269 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
270 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
271 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
272 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
273 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
274
275 int arc_counter = 0; // Counter for jump arcs added
276 int missing_counter = 0; // Counter for missing paths
277
278 // Loop through all buckets
279 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
280 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid bucket indices
281
282 // Cache the current bucket's node ID and arcs
283 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
284 const auto &arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
285 const auto &original_arcs = nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
286
287 if (arcs.empty()) { continue; } // Skip if no arcs in the current bucket
288
289 // Process each original arc for the current node
290 for (const auto &orig_arc : original_arcs) {
291 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
292 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
293 bool have_path = false;
294
295 // Check if the path exists in the current bucket arcs
296 for (const auto &gamma : arcs) {
297 if (fixed_buckets[gamma.from_bucket][gamma.to_bucket] == 1) { continue; }
298
299 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
300 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
301
302 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
303 have_path = true;
304 break; // Path found, exit early
305 }
306 }
307
308 // If no path was found, find a jump arc
309 if (!have_path) {
310 ++missing_counter; // Increment missing path counter
311
312 // Cache the starting bucket and the number of buckets for this node
313 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_index[current_node_id];
314 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
315
316 auto process_bucket = [&](int b, int b_prime) {
317 // Check if b_prime is adjacent to b in Phi
318 const auto &phi_b_prime = Phi[b_prime];
319 if (std::find(phi_b_prime.begin(), phi_b_prime.end(), b) == phi_b_prime.end()) {
320 return; // Not adjacent, skip
321 }
322
323 // Check arcs in the adjacent bucket b_prime
324 const auto &arcs_prime = buckets[b_prime].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
325 for (const auto &gamma_prime : arcs_prime) {
326 if (fixed_buckets[gamma_prime.from_bucket][gamma_prime.to_bucket] == 1) { continue; }
327
328 const int from_node_prime = buckets[gamma_prime.from_bucket].node_id;
329 const int to_node_prime = buckets[gamma_prime.to_bucket].node_id;
330
331 // If the arc matches, add a jump arc
332 if (from_node == from_node_prime && to_node == to_node_prime) {
333 B_bar.push_back(b_prime); // Add the valid bucket to B_bar
334
335 // Wrap the resource increment in a vector
336 const std::vector<double> res = gamma_prime.resource_increment;
337 const double cost = gamma_prime.cost_increment;
338
339 buckets[b].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, b_prime, res, cost);
340 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, b_prime, res, cost, to_node);
341 ++arc_counter; // Increment the jump arc counter
342 }
343 }
344 };
345
346 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
347 // Forward direction: incrementing buckets
348 for (int b_prime = b + 1; b_prime < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++b_prime) {
349 process_bucket(b, b_prime);
350 }
351 } else {
352 // Backward direction: decrementing buckets
353 // for (int b_prime = b - 1; b_prime >= start_bucket; --b_prime) { process_bucket(b, b_prime); }
354 for (int b_prime = b + 1; b_prime < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++b_prime) {
355 process_bucket(b, b_prime);
356 }
357 }
358 }
359 }
360 }
361 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
362 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
363}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)
Definition Definitions.h:358
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 795 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

795 {
796 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
797 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
798 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
799
800 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
801 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------------------------------+\n");
802 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset, " Forward", " Backward");
803 fmt::print("+----------------------------------------------------------+\n");
804
805 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
806 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset, stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
807
808 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
809 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
810
811 // Print the final horizontal line
812 fmt::print("+----------------------------------------------------------+\n");
813
814 fmt::print("\n");
815}
reset
constexpr auto reset

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma , typename VRPNode >
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 8 of file BucketRes.h.

10 {
11 for (size_t I = 0; I < N; ++I) {
12 if (!process_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources, gamma, theNode, I)) {
13 return false; // baldesCtr violated, return false
14 }
15 }
16 return true; // All resources processed successfully
17}

◆ process_buckets()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::process_buckets ( auto buckets_size,
auto & buckets,
auto & save_rebuild,
auto & fixed_buckets )
inline

Processes the buckets in a specific direction for heritages.

This function processes the buckets in the specified direction (Forward or Backward) to identify fixed arcs based on heritages. It iterates through each bucket and checks if the node exists in the save_rebuild map. If the node is found, it searches for arcs and updates the fixed buckets accordingly.

Definition at line 502 of file BucketGraph.h.

502 {
503 std::vector<int> tasks;
504 std::atomic<int> n_fixed = 0; // Declare n_fixed as atomic for thread safety
505
506 unsigned int total_threads = std::thread::hardware_concurrency();
507 // Divide by 2 to use only half of the available threads
508 unsigned int half_threads = 1;
509
510 const int JOBS = half_threads;
511 exec::static_thread_pool pool(JOBS);
512 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
513
514 // Create tasks for each bucket
515 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < buckets_size; ++bucket) { tasks.push_back(bucket); }
516
517 // Define chunk size to reduce parallelization overhead
518 const int chunk_size = 50; // You can adjust this based on performance experiments
519
520 // Parallel execution in chunks using NVIDIA stdexec
521 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
522 stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size, [&, chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
523 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
524 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
525
526 // Process a chunk of tasks (buckets)
527 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
528 int bucket = tasks[task_idx];
529 auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
530 auto &from_bucket = buckets[bucket];
531 BucketRange<D> from_range = {from_bucket.lb, from_bucket.ub};
532 auto from_node = from_bucket.node_id;
533
534 // Check if the node exists in the save_rebuild map (only once per bucket)
535 auto it = save_rebuild.find(from_node);
536 if (it != save_rebuild.end()) {
537 const auto &save_tree = it->second;
538
539 for (auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
540 auto to_bucket = arc.to_bucket;
541 auto to_node = buckets[to_bucket].node_id;
542 BucketRange<D> to_range = {buckets[to_bucket].lb, buckets[to_bucket].ub};
543
544 // Perform the search and update fixed buckets
545 auto is_contained = save_tree.search(from_range, to_range, to_node);
546 if (is_contained) {
547 fixed_buckets[bucket][to_bucket] = 1;
548 // Update fixed count in a thread-safe manner
549 std::atomic_fetch_add(&n_fixed, 1);
550 }
551 }
552 }
553 }
554 });
555
556 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
557 // Execute the bulk sender
558 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
559
560 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
561 print_info("[Fw] {} arcs fixed from heritages\n", n_fixed.load());
562 } else {
563 print_info("[Bw] {} arcs fixed from heritages\n", n_fixed.load());
564 }
565
566 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<D>();
567 }

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma , typename VRPNode >
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

Definition at line 28 of file BucketRes.h.

29 {
30 if (options.resource_type[I] == DISPOSABLE) { // Checked at compile-time
31 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
32 new_resource =
33 std::max(initial_resources[I] + gamma.resource_increment[I], static_cast<double>(theNode.lb[I]));
34 if (new_resource > theNode.ub[I]) {
35 return false; // Exceeds upper bound, return false to stop processing
36 }
37 } else {
38 new_resource =
39 std::min(initial_resources[I] - gamma.resource_increment[I], static_cast<double>(theNode.ub[I]));
40 if (new_resource < theNode.lb[I]) {
41 return false; // Below lower bound, return false to stop processing
42 }
43 }
44 } else if (options.resource_type[I] == NON_DISPOSABLE) {
45 // TODO: Non-disposable resource handling, check if it is right
46 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
47 new_resource = initial_resources[I] + gamma.resource_increment[I];
48 if (new_resource > theNode.ub[I]) {
49 return false; // Exceeds upper bound, return false to stop processing
50 } else if (new_resource < theNode.lb[I]) {
51 return false; // Below lower bound, return false to stop processing
52 }
53 } else {
54 new_resource = initial_resources[I] - gamma.resource_increment[I];
55 if (new_resource > theNode.ub[I]) {
56 return false; // Exceeds upper bound, return false to stop processing
57 } else if (new_resource < theNode.lb[I]) {
58 return false; // Below lower bound, return false to stop processing
59 }
60 }
61 } else if (options.resource_type[I] == BINARY) {
62 // TODO:: Binary resource handling, check if logic is right
63 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
64 // For binary resources, flip between 0 and 1 based on gamma.resource_increment[I]
65 if (gamma.resource_increment[I] > 0) {
66 new_resource = 1.0; // Switch "on"
67 } else {
68 new_resource = 0.0; // Switch "off"
69 }
70 } else {
71 // In reverse, toggle as well
72 if (gamma.resource_increment[I] > 0) {
73 new_resource = 0.0; // Reverse logic: turn "off"
74 } else {
75 new_resource = 1.0; // Reverse logic: turn "on"
76 }
77 }
78 } else if (options.resource_type[I] == MTW) {
79 // TODO: handling multiple time windows case
80 // "OR" resource case using mtw_lb and mtw_ub vectors for multiple time windows
81 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
82 bool is_feasible = false;
83 for (size_t i = 0; i < theNode.mtw_lb.size(); ++i) {
84 new_resource = std::max(initial_resources[I] + gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.mtw_lb[i]);
85 if (new_resource > theNode.ub[I]) {
86 continue; // Exceeds upper bound, try next time window
87 } else {
88 is_feasible = true; // Feasible in this time window
89 break;
90 }
91 }
92
93 if (!is_feasible) {
94 return false; // Not feasible in any of the ranges
95 }
96
97 return true; // Successfully processed all resources
98 } else {
99 bool is_feasible = false;
100 for (size_t i = 0; i < theNode.mtw_ub.size(); ++i) {
101 new_resource = std::min(initial_resources[I] - gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.mtw_ub[i]);
102 if (new_resource < theNode.lb[I]) {
103 continue; // Below lower bound, try next time window }
104 } else {
105 is_feasible = true; // Feasible in this time window break;
106 }
107 }
108
109 if (!is_feasible) {
110 return false; // Not feasible in any of the ranges
111 }
112 }
113 } else if (options.resource_type[I] == BATTERY_RESOURCE) {
114 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
115 if (theNode.is_station) {
116 new_resource = options.battery_capacity; // Full recharge
117 } else {
118 new_resource = initial_resources[I];// - gamma.energy_consumption;
119 }
120 if (new_resource < 0) return false;
121 } else {
122 // Backward direction analogous but reversed
123 if (theNode.is_station) {
124 new_resource = options.battery_capacity;
125 } else {
126 new_resource = initial_resources[I];// + gamma.energy_consumption;
127 }
128 if (new_resource > options.battery_capacity) return false;
129 }
130 }
131 else if (options.resource_type[I] == RECHARGE_COUNT) {
132 if (theNode.is_station) {
133 new_resource = initial_resources[I] + 1;
134 if (new_resource > options.max_recharges) return false;
135 } else {
136 new_resource = initial_resources[I];
137 }
138 }
139 else if (options.resource_type[I] == RECHARGE_TIME) {
140 if (theNode.is_station) {
141 new_resource = 0;//gamma.recharge_time;
142 } else {
143 new_resource = initial_resources[I];// + gamma.recharge_time;
144 if (new_resource > options.max_recharge_time) return false;
145 }
146 }
147 return true; // Successfully processed all resources
148}

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

◆ reset_pool()

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

This function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1057 of file BucketSolve.h.

1058 {
1059 // Create tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms
1060
1061 auto forward_task =
1062 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1063 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1064 });
1065
1066 auto backward_task =
1067 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1068 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1069 });
1070
1071 // Execute the tasks in parallel and synchronize
1072 auto work =
1073 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1074 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1075 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1076 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1077 });
1078
1079 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
1080}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ SCC_handler()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::SCC_handler ( )

Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.

This function processes the bucket graph to identify SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm. It extends the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets, computes the SCCs, and orders them topologically. It also sorts the buckets within each SCC based on their lower or upper bounds, depending on the direction. Finally, it splits the arcs for each SCC and removes duplicates.

Definition at line 569 of file BucketUtils.h.

569 {
570 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
571 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
572 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
573 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph = bucket_graph;
574
575 // Extend the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets
576 for (auto i = 0; i < extended_bucket_graph.size(); ++i) {
577 auto phi_set = Phi[i];
578 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
579 for (auto &phi_bucket : phi_set) { extended_bucket_graph[phi_bucket].push_back(i); }
580 }
581
582 SCC scc_finder;
583 scc_finder.convertFromUnorderedMap(extended_bucket_graph); // print extended bucket graph
584
585 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
586 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
587
588#ifdef VERBOSE
589 // print SCCs and buckets in it
590 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
591 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
592 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
593 fmt::print("FW SCCs: \n");
594 } else {
595 fmt::print("BW SCCs: \n");
596 }
597 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
598 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
599 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
600 } else {
601 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
602 }
603 for (auto &bucket : sccs[scc]) { fmt::print("{} ", bucket); }
604 }
605 fmt::print("\n");
606#endif
607
608 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
609 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size()); // Reserve space for all SCCs
610 for (int i : topological_order) { ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[i]); }
611
612 auto sorted_sccs = sccs;
613 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
614 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
615 std::sort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].lb[0] < buckets[b].lb[0]; });
616 } else {
617 std::sort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].ub[0] > buckets[b].ub[0]; });
618 }
619 }
620
621 // iterate over nodes
622 for (auto &node : nodes) {
623 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
624 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
625 } else {
626 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
627 }
628 }
629
630 // Split arcs for each SCC
631 auto scc_ctr = 0;
632 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
633 // Iterate over each bucket in the SCC
634 for (int bucket : scc) {
635 int from_node_id = buckets[bucket].node_id; // Get the source node ID
636 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id]; // Access the corresponding node
637 // Define filtered arcs depending on the direction
638 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
639 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_fw_arcs = nodes[from_node_id].fw_arcs_scc[scc_ctr]; // For forward direction
640
641 // Iterate over the arcs from the current bucket
642 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
643 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
644 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id; // Get the destination node ID
645
646 // Search for the arc from `from_node_id` to `to_node_id` in the node's arcs
647 auto it = std::find_if(node.fw_arcs.begin(), node.fw_arcs.end(),
648 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
649
650 // If both nodes are within the current SCC, retain the arc
651 if (it != node.fw_arcs.end()) {
652 // Add the arc to the filtered arcs
653 filtered_fw_arcs.push_back(*it); // Forward arcs
654 }
655 }
656 } else {
657 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_bw_arcs = nodes[from_node_id].bw_arcs_scc[scc_ctr]; // For forward direction
658
659 // Iterate over the arcs from the current bucket
660 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
661 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
662 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id; // Get the destination node ID
663
664 // Search for the arc from `from_node_id` to `to_node_id` in the node's arcs
665 auto it = std::find_if(node.bw_arcs.begin(), node.bw_arcs.end(),
666 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
667
668 // If both nodes are within the current SCC, retain the arc
669 if (it != node.bw_arcs.end()) {
670 // Add the arc to the filtered arcs
671 filtered_bw_arcs.push_back(*it); // Forward arcs
672 }
673 }
674 }
675 }
676
677 // Increment SCC counter
678 ++scc_ctr;
679 }
680
681 for (auto &node : nodes) {
682 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
683 // Iterate over all SCCs for each node
684 for (auto &fw_arcs_scc : node.fw_arcs_scc) {
685 // Sort arcs by from_bucket and to_bucket
686 std::sort(fw_arcs_scc.begin(), fw_arcs_scc.end(),
687 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to); });
688
689 // Remove consecutive duplicates
690 auto last = std::unique(fw_arcs_scc.begin(), fw_arcs_scc.end(),
691 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to; });
692
693 // Erase the duplicates from the vector
694 fw_arcs_scc.erase(last, fw_arcs_scc.end());
695 }
696 } else {
697 // Iterate over all SCCs for each node
698 for (auto &bw_arcs_scc : node.bw_arcs_scc) {
699 // Sort arcs by from_bucket and to_bucket
700 std::sort(bw_arcs_scc.begin(), bw_arcs_scc.end(),
701 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to); });
702
703 // Remove consecutive duplicates
704 auto last = std::unique(bw_arcs_scc.begin(), bw_arcs_scc.end(),
705 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to; });
706
707 // Erase the duplicates from the vector
708 bw_arcs_scc.erase(last, bw_arcs_scc.end());
709 }
710 }
711 }
712
713 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
714 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
715 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
716 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
717 fw_sccs = sccs;
718 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
719 fw_union_find = uf;
720 } else {
721 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
722 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
723 bw_sccs = sccs;
724 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
725 bw_union_find = uf;
726 }
727}
blue
constexpr const char * blue

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 915 of file BucketUtils.h.

915 {
916 // Clear existing arcs for each node
917 for (auto &node : nodes) {
918 node.clear_arcs(); // Remove any existing arcs associated with the node
919 }
920
921 /*
922 RawArcList heur_arcs;
923 for (const auto &path : topHeurRoutes) {

926 int to = path[i + 1];
927 RawArc arc(from, to);

929 }
930 }
931 */
932 // Step 1: Compute the clusters using MST-based clustering
933 MST mst_solver(nodes, [&](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
934 double theta = 1.0; // Experiment with different values of θ
935 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
936
937 // Create a job-to-cluster mapping (cluster ID for each job/node)
938 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1); // Mapping from job (node) to cluster ID
939 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
940 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) { job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id; }
941 }
942
943 // Step 2: Modify add_arcs_for_node to give priority based on cluster membership
944 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket, std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
945 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>,
946 double>; // Arc: priority, to_node, resource increments, cost increment
947
948 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs;
949 best_arcs.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for forward arcs
950
951 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs_rev;
952 best_arcs_rev.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for reverse arcs
953
954 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
955 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id) continue; // Skip depot and same node
956
957 if (options.pstep == true) {
958 if (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot || next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot)
959 continue; // Skip depot and end depot
960 }
961
962 auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id); // Calculate travel cost
963 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost; // Adjust cost increment by subtracting next node's cost
964
965 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
966 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
967 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
968 res_inc[r] = travel_cost + node.duration; // Update resource increment based on node duration
969 } else {
970 res_inc[r] = node.duration / 2 + travel_cost +
971 next_node.duration / 2; // Update resource increment based on node duration
972 }
973 } else {
974 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
975 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
976 } else {
977 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r] / 2 + next_node.consumption[r] / 2;
978 }
979 }
980 }
981
982 int to_bucket = next_node.id;
983 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
984
985 bool feasible = true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints
986 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
987 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
988 feasible = false;
989 break;
990 }
991 }
992 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
993
994 // Step 3: Calculate priority based on cluster membership
995 double priority_value;
996 double reverse_priority_value;
997
998 // bool is_heuristic_arc = heur_arcs.has_arc(node.id, next_node.id);
999
1000 if (job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]) {
1001 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster
1002 priority_value = 5.0 + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Adjust weight for same-cluster priority
1003 reverse_priority_value = 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for same-cluster priority
1004 } else {
1005 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
1006 priority_value = 1.0 + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for cross-cluster arcs
1007 reverse_priority_value = 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for cross-cluster arcs
1008 }
1009 best_arcs.emplace_back(priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
1010 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
1011 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
1012 }
1013
1014 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
1015 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
1016 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
1017 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
1018 priority_value); // Add forward arc
1019 // fmt::print("Node ID: {}, To Bucket: {}, Cost Inc: {}\n", node.id, to_bucket, cost_inc);
1020 }
1021
1022 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node
1023 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs_rev) {
1024 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
1025 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
1026 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
1027 }
1028 };
1029
1030 // Step 4: Iterate over all nodes to set the adjacency list
1031 // print depot and end depot
1032 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
1033 // fmt::print("Node ID: {}\n", VRPNode.id);
1034
1035 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot) {
1036 continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
1037 }
1038
1039 std::vector<double> res_inc(intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
1040 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id, res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
1041 }
1042}

void BucketGraph::set_distance_matrix ( const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & distanceMatrix,

Sets the distance matrix and calculates neighborhoods.

This function assigns the provided distance matrix to the internal distance matrix of the class and then calculates the neighborhoods based on the given number of nearest neighbors.

Marks a node as unreachable in the given bitmap.

This function sets the bit corresponding to the specified node_id in the bitmap, indicating that the node is unreachable.

◆ solve()

template<Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solve ( bool trigger = false)
inline

Solves the bucket graph optimization problem using a multi-stage bi-labeling algorithm.

The function iteratively adjusts the terminal time and handles different stages of the bi-labeling algorithm to find the optimal paths. The stages are adaptive and transition based on the inner objective values and iteration counts.

Definition at line 52 of file BucketSolve.h.

52 {
53 // Initialize the status as not optimal at the start
54 status = Status::NotOptimal;
55 if (trigger) {
56 transition = true;
57 fixed = false;
58 }
59
60 updateSplit(); // Update the split values for the bucket graph
61
62 // Placeholder for the final paths (labels) and inner objective value
63 std::vector<Label *> paths;
64
66 // ADAPTIVE STAGE HANDLING
68 // Stage 1: Apply a light heuristic (Stage::One)
69 if (s1 && depth == 0) {
70 stage = 1;
71 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>(); // Solve the problem with
72 // Stage 1 heuristic
73 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
74
75 // Transition from Stage 1 to Stage 2 if the objective improves or after
76 // 10 iterations
77 if (inner_obj >= -1 || iter >= 10) {
78 s1 = false;
79 s2 = true; // Move to Stage 2
80 }
81 }
82 // Stage 2: Apply a more expensive pricing heuristic (Stage::Two)
83 else if (s2 && depth == 0) {
84 s2 = true;
85 stage = 2;
86 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Two>(); // Solve the problem with
87 // Stage 2 heuristic
88 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
89
90 // Transition from Stage 2 to Stage 3 if the objective improves or after
91 // 800 iterations
92 if (inner_obj >= -100 || iter > 500) {
93 s2 = false;
94 s3 = true; // Move to Stage 3
95 }
96 }
97 // Stage 3: Apply a heuristic fixing approach (Stage::Three)
98 else if (s3 && depth == 0) {
99 stage = 3;
100 paths =
101 bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Three>(); // Solve the problem with
102 // Stage 3 heuristic
103 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
104
105 // Transition from Stage 3 to Stage 4 if the objective improves
106 // significantly
107 if (inner_obj >= -0.5) {
108 s4 = true;
109 s3 = false;
110 transition = true; // Prepare for transition to Stage 4
111 }
112 }
113 // Stage 4: Exact labeling algorithm with fixing enabled (Stage::Four)
114 else {
115 stage = 4;
116
117#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
118 // If transitioning to Stage 4, print a message and apply fixing
119 if (transition) {
120 // print_cut("Transitioning to stage 4\n");
121 bool original_fixed = fixed; // Store the original fixed status
122 fixed = true; // Enable fixing of buckets in Stage 4
123 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>(); // Solve the problem
124 // with Stage 4
125 transition = false; // End the transition period
126 fixed = original_fixed; // Restore the original fixed status
127 min_red_cost = paths[0]->cost; // Update the minimum reduced cost
128 iter++;
129 return paths; // Return the final paths
130 }
131#endif
132 // If not transitioning, continue with Stage 4 algorithm
133 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();
134 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
135
136 auto rollback =
137 updateStepSize(); // Update the step size for the bucket graph
138 // auto rollback = false;
139 // auto rollback = false;
140 if (rollback) {
141 // s4 = false; // Rollback to Stage 3 if necessary
142 // s3 = true;
143 status = Status::Rollback; // Set status to rollback
144 return paths; // Return the paths after rollback
145 }
146 // If the objective improves sufficiently, set the status to separation
147 // or optimal
148 if (inner_obj >= -1.0) {
149 ss = true; // Enter separation mode (for SRC handling)
150#if !defined(SRC) && !defined(SRC3)
151 status = Status::Optimal; // If SRC is not defined, set status to
152 // optimal
153 return paths; // Return the optimal paths
154#endif
155 status = Status::Separation; // If SRC is defined, set status to
156 // separation
157 }
158 }
159
160 iter++; // Increment the iteration counter
161
162 return paths; // Return the final paths after processing
163}

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

Definition at line 24 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

24 {
25
26 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
27
28 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
29 std::vector<double> all_costs;
30 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
31
32 // Mutex for shared resources
33 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
34
35 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
36 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
37 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
38
39 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
40 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
41 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
42 if (i == j) continue;
43 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
44 }
45 }
46 auto bulk_sender =
47 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
48 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
49 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
50 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
51 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
52 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
53 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
54 BucketOptions sub_options;
55 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
56 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
57 sub_options.depot = i;
58 sub_options.end_depot = j;
59 // print i and j
60 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
61 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
62 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
63 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
64 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
65 sub_bg.setup();
66 sub_bg.reset_pool();
67 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
68 // Solve and get the paths
69 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
70 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
71 std::vector<Label *> paths;
72 // print paths.size
73 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
74 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
75 // print bucket_labels size
76 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
77 if (!label) continue;
78 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
79 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
80 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
81 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
82 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
83 paths.push_back(new_label);
84 }
85 }
86 }
87
88 // Check if no paths are found
89 if (paths.empty()) return;
90
91 // Lock to protect shared resources
92 {
93 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
94 // Process the found paths
95 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
96 }
97 // }
98 }
99 });
100
101 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
102 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
103 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
104
105 return all_paths;
106}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP_by_MTZ ( )

Definition at line 356 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

356 {
357 int JOBS = nodes.size();
358
359 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
360 std::vector<double> all_costs;
361 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
362 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
363
364 auto duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, true);
365 auto max_pstep_iterations = 10;
366 for (auto z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
367 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
368 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
369 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
370 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
371 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
372 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
373
374 // Convert three_two to vector of pairs
375 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
376 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
377 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
378 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
379 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
380
381 // Convert three_three to vector of pairs
382 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
383 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
384 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
385 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
386 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
387
388 // Convert three_five to vector of pairs of pairs
389 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
390 size_t total_size = 0;
391 for (const auto &row : three_five_duals) total_size += row.size();
392 arc_duals_tuples.reserve(total_size);
393
394 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
395 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
396 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
397 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
398 }
399 }
400 }
401 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
402
403 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
404 // print sub_paths.size
405 auto paths_added = 0;
406 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
407 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
408 paths_added++;
409 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
410 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
411 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
412 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
413 }
414 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
415
416 duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix);
417 }
418 return {};
419}

◆ solveTSP()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSP ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 228 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

230 {
231 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
232 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsp", 0, 0);
233 if (first_time) { generateInitialPaths(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix); }
234 // Define variables
235 int R = paths.size(); // Number of p-steps
236 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for each p-step
237 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> u(n); // Continuous variables for each node
238
239 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
240 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
241 // fmt::print("path_costs[{}] = {}\n", i, path_costs[i]);
242 std::string varname = "x_" + std::to_string(i);
243 double custo = path_costs[i];
244 x[i] = model.add_variable(varname, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1.0, custo);
245 }
246
247 // Create continuous variables u_i for each node i
248 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
249 auto name = "u_" + std::to_string(i);
250 u[i] = model.add_variable(name, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, n - 1, 0.0);
251 }
252 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
253
254 // Constraints (3.3) and (3.4):
255 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
256 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
257 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
258 LinearExpression in_constraint;
259 LinearExpression out_constraint;
260 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
261 if (firsts[r] == i) { in_constraint += x[r]; }
262 if (lasts[r] == i) { in_constraint -= x[r]; }
263 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end() && i != lasts[r]) {
264 out_constraint += x[r];
265 }
266 }
267 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(in_constraint == 0, "33_" + std::to_string(i));
268 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(out_constraint == 1, "34_" + std::to_string(i));
269 }
270
271 // Constraints (3.5): Avoid sub-tours using MTZ constraints
272 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
273 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
274 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
275 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
276 if (i != j) {
277 LinearExpression r_sum;
278 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
279 for (int k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
280 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { r_sum += x[r]; }
281 }
282 }
283 auto ctr = u[i] - u[j] + (n - 1) * r_sum <= (n - 2);
284 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] =
285 model.add_constraint(ctr, "mtz_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
286 }
287 }
288 }
289
290 for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
291 model.add_constraint(u[i] >= 0, "u_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
292 model.add_constraint(u[i] <= n - 1, "u_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
293 }
294
295#ifdef IPM
296 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
297 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
298 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
299
300 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
301#else
302 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
303#endif
304 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
305 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
306 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
307 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
308 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
309 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
310 }
311
312 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
313 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
314 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
315 if (i != j) {
316 three_five_duals[i][j] =
317 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
318 }
319 }
320 }
321#ifdef IPM
322 auto lp_obj = ipSolver->getObjective();
323#else
324 auto lp_obj = 0.0;
325#endif
326 fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", lp_obj);
327 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
328 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release(); // Release ownership from unique_ptr
329 // set model verbose
330 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 0);
331 // set model type as 2
332 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 2);
333
334 // convert x vars to int and solve again
335 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
336 modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).set(GRB_CharAttr_VType, GRB_BINARY);
337 }
338 modelGRB->update();
339 modelGRB->optimize();
340
341 if (modelGRB->get(GRB_IntAttr_Status) == GRB_OPTIMAL) {
342 fmt::print("Integer solution found with value: {}\n", modelGRB->get(GRB_DoubleAttr_ObjVal));
343 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
344 if (modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X) > 0.5) {
345 fmt::print("x_{} = {}\n", i, modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X));
346 // print path nodes
347 for (auto node : paths[i]) { fmt::print("{} ", node); }
348 fmt::print("\n");
349 }
350 }
351 }
352
353 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
354}

◆ solveTSPTW()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSPTW ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
std::vector< double > & service_times,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_start,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_end,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 421 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

425 {
426 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
427 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsptw", 0, 0);
428
429 if (first_time) {
430 generateInitialPathsTSPTW(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix, time_windows_start,
431 time_windows_end, service_times);
432 }
433
434 // Variables
435 int R = paths.size();
436 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for p-steps
437 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> omega(n); // Continuous variables for accumulated time
438
439 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
440 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
441 x[i] = model.add_variable("x_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Binary, 0.0, 1.0, path_costs[i]);
442 }
443
444 // Create continuous variables ω_i for time accumulation
445 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
446 omega[i] = model.add_variable("omega_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1e8, 0.0);
447 }
448 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
449
450 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
451 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
452 // Constraints (4.11): Flow balance for p-steps
453 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
454 LinearExpression flow_balance;
455 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
456 if (firsts[r] == i) flow_balance += x[r];
457 if (lasts[r] == i) flow_balance -= x[r];
458 }
459 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(flow_balance == 0, "flow_" + std::to_string(i));
460 }
461
462 // Constraints (4.12): Visit each node at most once
463 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
464 LinearExpression visit_once;
465 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
466 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { visit_once += x[r]; }
467 }
468 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(visit_once <= 1, "visit_" + std::to_string(i));
469 }
470
471 // Constraint (4.13): Visit source exactly once
472 LinearExpression source_visit;
473 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
474 if (firsts[r] == 0) { source_visit += x[r]; }
475 }
476 model.add_constraint(source_visit == 1, "source");
477
478 // Constraints (4.14): Time accumulation with big-M
479 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
480 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
481 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
482 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
483 if (i != j) {
484 double M_ij = 1e8;
485 LinearExpression time_acc;
486 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
487 for (size_t k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
488 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { time_acc += x[r]; }
489 }
490 }
491 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] = model.add_constraint(
492 omega[j] - omega[i] - (cost_matrix[i][j] + service_times[i]) * time_acc - M_ij * time_acc >= -M_ij,
493 "time_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
494 }
495 }
496 }
497
498 // define omega[0] = 0
499
500 // Constraints (4.15): Time window constraints
501 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
502 LinearExpression node_used;
503 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
504 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { node_used += x[r]; }
505 }
506 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_start[i] * node_used >= 0, "tw_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
507 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_end[i] * node_used <= 0, "tw_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
508 }
509
510// Solve the model
511#ifdef IPM
512 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
513 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
514 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
515
516 // Extract solution and duals
517 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
518#else
519 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
520#endif
521 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
522 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
523 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
524 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
525 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
526 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
527 fmt::print("Duals for node {}: 3.2 = {}, 3.3 = {}\n", i, three_two_duals[i], three_three_duals[i]);
528 }
529
530 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
531 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
532 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
533 if (i != j) {
534 three_five_duals[i][j] =
535 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
536 }
537 }
538 }
539
540 // Solution output and return
541 // fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", ipSolver->getObjective());
542
543 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
544
545 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release();
546 // set model verbose
547 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 1);
548 // set model type as 2
549 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 0);
550
551 modelGRB->update();
552 modelGRB->optimize();
553
554 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
555}
generateInitialPathsTSPTW
void generateInitialPathsTSPTW(int n, std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, const std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix, const std::vector< double > &start_times, const std::vector< double > &end_times, const std::vector< double > &service_times)

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solveTSPTW_by_MTZ ( )

Definition at line 557 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

557 {
558 int JOBS = nodes.size();
559
560 // Initialize containers for storing paths, costs, firsts, and lasts
561 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
562 std::vector<double> all_costs;
563 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
564 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
565
566 // extract service time from nodes
567 std::vector<double> service_times;
568 std::vector<double> time_windows_start(JOBS, 0.0);
569 std::vector<double> time_windows_end(JOBS, 10000.0);
570 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS; ++i) {
571 time_windows_start[i] = nodes[i].lb[0];
572 time_windows_end[i] = nodes[i].ub[0];
573 service_times.push_back(nodes[i].duration);
574 }
575 // Step 1: Solve the initial TSPTW using the previously defined method
576 auto duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
577 time_windows_start, time_windows_end, true);
578
579 // Step 2: Set up iteration for generating new paths using dual values
580 int max_pstep_iterations = 10;
581 for (int z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
582 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
583 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
584
585 // Extract dual values for constraints
586 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
587 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
588 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
589 // auto arrival_time_duals = std::get<3>(duals);
590
591 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
592
593 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.32)
594 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
595 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
596 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
597 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
598 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
599
600 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.33)
601 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
602 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
603 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
604 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
605 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
606
607 // Convert dual values for MTZ constraints (3.34) and time windows (3.35)
608 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
609 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
610 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
611 if (i != j) { arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]); }
612 }
613 }
614 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
615
616 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
617 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
618 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
619 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
620 }
621 }
622 }
623 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
624
625 // Step 3: Solve the subproblem to generate new p-steps
626 fmt::print("Solving PSTEP subproblem\n");
627 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
628 fmt::print("Subproblem solved\n");
629
630 // Add the new paths to the existing list if valid
631 int paths_added = 0;
632 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
633 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
634 paths_added++;
635 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
636 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
637 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
638 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
639 }
640
641 // If no new paths were added, stop the iteration
642 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
643
644 // Step 4: Re-solve the TSPTW with the updated set of p-steps
645 duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
646 time_windows_start, time_windows_end);
647 }
648
649 return {};
650}

auto solveTSPTW(std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > &paths, std::vector< double > &path_costs, std::vector< int > &firsts, std::vector< int > &lasts, std::vector< std::vector< double > > &cost_matrix, std::vector< double > &service_times, std::vector< double > &time_windows_start, std::vector< double > &time_windows_end, bool first_time=false)

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::split_buckets ( )
inline

Definition at line 627 of file BucketGraph.h.

627 {
628 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
629 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
630 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
631 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
632
633 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
634 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
635 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
636
637 // Determine the base interval and other relevant values for each resource
638 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
639 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
640 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
641 }
642
643 int original_num_buckets = buckets_size;
644
645 // To track splits per node_id
646 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, int> splits_per_node;
647
648 // Loop until the second-to-last bucket to avoid out-of-bounds access
649 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets - 1; ++i) {
650 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
651
652 // Calculate mid-point for each dimension
653 std::vector<double> mid_point(bucket.lb.size());
654 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
655 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) { mid_point[r] = (bucket.lb[r] + base_intervals[r]) / 2.0; }
656 } else {
657 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) { mid_point[r] = (bucket.ub[r] - base_intervals[r]) / 2.0; }
658 }
659
660 // Create two new buckets by splitting at the mid-point
661 Bucket bucket1, bucket2;
662 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
663 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
664 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, mid_point, bucket.ub);
665 } else {
666 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
667 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, mid_point);
668 }
669
670 // Manually shift elements to the right to make space for the new bucket
671 for (int j = original_num_buckets; j > i + 1; --j) { buckets[j] = buckets[j - 1]; }
672
673 // Insert the new bucket at position i+1
674 buckets[i + 1] = bucket2;
675
676 // Replace the current bucket with the first half (bucket1)
677 buckets[i] = bucket1;
678
679 // Track the number of splits for this node_id
680 splits_per_node[bucket.node_id]++;
681
682 // Since we added a new bucket at i+1, increment i to skip over the new bucket
683 ++i;
684 ++original_num_buckets; // Adjust the count to account for the new bucket
685 }
686
687 // Update the global bucket size
688 buckets_size = original_num_buckets;
689
690 // Now update the num_buckets_index for each node_id based on the final positions
691 int current_index = 0;
692 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets; ++i) {
693 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
694 if (num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] == -1) { num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] = current_index; }
695 current_index++;
696 }
697 }

◆ UpdateBucketsSet()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::UpdateBucketsSet ( double theta,
const Label * label,
ankerl::unordered_dense::set< int > & Bbidi,
int & current_bucket,
std::vector< uint64_t > & Bvisited )

Updates the set of buckets based on the given parameters.

This function inserts the current bucket into the visited set and then performs various checks and updates based on the provided theta, label, Bbidi, current_bucket, and Bvisited parameters.

Definition at line 36 of file BucketJump.h.

37 {
38 // Precompute values and assign references for the direction-specific data
39
40 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
41 auto &buckets_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_buckets, fw_buckets);
42 auto &c_bar_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_c_bar, fw_c_bar);
43
44 const int bucketLnode = label->node_id;
45 std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
46 bucket_stack.reserve(10); // Pre-allocate space for the stack
47 bucket_stack.push_back(current_bucket);
48
49 // Lambda to compare bitmaps
50 auto bitmaps_conflict = [&](const Label *L1, const Label *L2) {
51 for (size_t i = 0; i < L1->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
52 if ((L1->visited_bitmap[i] & L2->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) { return true; }
53 }
54 return false;
55 };
56
57 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
58 int curr_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
59 bucket_stack.pop_back();
60 const size_t segment = curr_bucket >> 6; // Determine the segment for the current bucket
61 const size_t bit_position = curr_bucket & 63; // Determine the bit position within the segment
62 Bvisited[segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position); // Set the bit corresponding to the current bucket as visited
63 // print current bucket
64 const int bucketLprimenode = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].node_id;
65 double cost = getcij(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
66
67 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(auto arc_dual = arc_duals.getDual(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode); cost -= arc_dual;)
68 // Stop exploring if the cost exceeds the threshold
69 if (label->cost + cost + c_bar_opposite[curr_bucket] >= theta) { continue; }
70
71 // Only process if bucket is not already in Bbidi
72 if (Bbidi.insert(curr_bucket).second) {
73 const auto opposite_labels = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].get_labels();
74 for (const auto &L : opposite_labels) {
75 if (label->node_id == L->node_id || bitmaps_conflict(label, L)) {
76 continue; // Skip labels with the same node ID or conflicting bitmaps
77 }
78
79 const VRPNode &L_last_node = (D == Direction::Forward) ? nodes[label->node_id] : nodes[L->node_id];
80
81 bool violated = false;
82 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
83 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
84 const double time_constraint = (D == Direction::Forward)
85 ? label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + L_last_node.duration
86 : L->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + L_last_node.duration;
87
88 if (time_constraint >
89 ((D == Direction::Forward) ? L->resources[TIME_INDEX] : label->resources[TIME_INDEX])) {
90 violated = true;
91 break;
92 }
93 } else {
94 const double resource_constraint = (D == Direction::Forward)
95 ? label->resources[r] + L_last_node.consumption[r]
96 : L->resources[r] + L_last_node.consumption[r];
97
98 if (resource_constraint > ((D == Direction::Forward) ? L->resources[r] : label->resources[r])) {
99 violated = true;
100 break;
101 }
102 }
103 }
104
105 if (violated) { continue; }
106
107 // Insert the current bucket into Bbidi if the total cost is below theta
108 if (label->cost + cost + L->cost < theta) { Bbidi.emplace(curr_bucket); }
109 }
110 }
111
112 // Add neighboring buckets to the stack, only if not visited
113 for (int b_prime : Phi_opposite[curr_bucket]) {
114 const size_t segment_prime = b_prime >> 6; // Determine the segment for the neighboring bucket
115 const size_t bit_position_prime = b_prime & 63; // Determine the bit position within the segment
116
117 // If the neighboring bucket hasn't been visited, push it onto the stack
118 if ((Bvisited[segment_prime] & (1ULL << bit_position_prime)) == 0) { bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime); }
119 }
120 }
121}

void BucketGraph::updateSplit ( )

790 {
792 // ADAPTIVE TERMINAL TIME
794 // Adjust the terminal time dynamically based on the difference between the number of forward and
795 // backward labels
796 for (auto &split : q_star) {
797 // print n_fw_labels and n_bw_labels
798 // If there are more backward labels than forward labels, increase the terminal time slightly
799 if (((static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels) - static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels)) /
800 static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels)) > 0.2) {
801 split += 0.05 * R_max[options.main_resources[0]];
802 }
803 // If there are more forward labels than backward labels, decrease the terminal time slightly
804 else if (((static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels) - static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels)) /
805 static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels)) > 0.2) {
806 split -= 0.05 * R_max[options.main_resources[0]];
807 }
808 }
809 }

bool BucketGraph::updateStepSize ( )

813 {
814 bool updated = false;
815 if (bucket_interval >= 100) { return false; }

819 mean_dominance_checks += dominance_checks_per_bucket[i];

823

826 bucket_interval = bucket_interval + 25;
827 redefine(bucket_interval);

829 fixed = false;
